commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c4c7b2108a46defb3986e45c71f5dd658962f27e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds no-op returns for same-content `write` calls and identical old/new `edit` inputs, plus an updated write-tool test.

PR surface: Source +24, Tests +8. Total +32 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can detect identical write content but still reports success, and edit no-change remains an unstructured failure path. I did not run a live agent-loop replay in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor proof plus code changes for terminal no-op classification and mixed edit semantics; automation should not be queued while external real behavior proof is missing.

target.title: Fix/no op file mutations
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where agents can repeatedly call `write` with identical content or `edit` with identical old/new text, and the tool reports success each time. The agent interprets this as progress and keeps retrying the same no-op mutation indefinitely, wasting tokens and inflating the tool loop without making any actual change.

## Why This Change Was Made

The `write` tool already had a content comparison precheck (`readOriginalWriteState`) that could detect identical content, but it was not used to short-circuit the write. Even when `precheck.state === "same"`, the tool would write the file again and return "Successfully wrote N bytes".

Similarly, the `edit` tool had no check for edits where `oldText === newText`. When passed an edit with identical before/after text, it would apply a no-op edit and still report "Successfully replaced N block(s)".

The fix:
1. **write.ts**: After `readOriginalWriteState` detects identical content (`state === "same"`), return immediately with a "No changes" result instead of writing the file.
2. **edit.ts**: Before reading or applying edits, check if any edit has `oldText === newText`. If so, return a "No changes" result instead of proceeding with the mutation.

Both changes return a clear text result (not an error) so the tool loop knows no progress was made, without breaking callers that expect a successful status code.

Non-goals:
- This does not add a generic circuit-breaker or retry budget — that remains a separate concern tracked in other issues.
- This does not change behavior for real writes/edits: different content still writes normally, new files still create normally.

## User Impact

Agents will no longer waste tokens re-writing identical file content or re-editing with identical old/new text. When a write or edit would produce no change, the tool returns a "No changes" message, allowing the agent to recognize it made no progress and move on.

## Evidence

### Test results

```
 ✓ |agents-support| write tool > writes file URL paths through path resolver (18ms)
 ✓ |agents-support| write tool > returns no-op when content already matches instead of throwing (4ms)
 ✓ |agents-support| write tool > recovers timeout-like post-write errors (2ms)
 ✓ |agents-support| write tool > recovers success after post-write abort (2ms)
 ✓ |agents-support| edit tool > ... (all passed)
```

- `write` with identical content returns "No changes: content is identical to existing ..." instead of writing the file
- `write` with different content still writes successfully (regression test preserved)
- `write` to a new file still creates it (regression test preserved)
- `edit` with identical old/new text returns "No changes: one or more edits have identical old and new text in ..."
- Existing `recoverSuccessfulWrite` behavior for post-write timeouts is unchanged
- Existing test for aborted signal with matching content updated to expect the no-op result instead of throwing

### Changes

| File | Lines | Change |
|------|-------|--------|
| `src/agents/sessions/tools/write.ts` | +11 | Return no-op result when `readOriginalWriteState` detects identical content |
| `src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.ts` | +13 | Return no-op result when any edit has `oldText === newText` |
| `src/agents/sessions/tools/write.test.ts` | +13/-5 | Update test to match no-op behavior |

Closes #96983
target.author: wm0018
